title: Calobota saharae
taxonomy: Calobota saharae is classified within the family Polygalaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as milkworts or polygalas. Calobota saharae is a species of flowering plant native to southern Africa. The genus Calobota is part of the Polygalaceae family, and Calobota saharae is one of the species within this genus. Calobota saharae is a succulent plant with unique characteristics.

intro: Calobota saharae, also known as the Sahara calobota, is a perennial plant species belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antidiabetic properties. The bioactive constituents of Calobota saharae include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. The most common herbal preparations made with Calobota saharae are infusions and decoctions, which are used to treat fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
